title: nginx returns 502 Bad Gateway to the backend
description_md:
A reverse proxy (nginx on port 8080) sits in front of a backend app. Users now get `502 Bad Gateway`. The backend process itself is healthy. Someone changed a port during a refactor and only updated one side. SSH in as `trainee` (passwordless `sudo`), find the mismatch between where nginx forwards and where the backend actually listens, and fix the proxy so requests get through.

You're done when `curl http://localhost:8080/` returns the backend's page.
